title: Foreign Currency Reserves 2026 – Market Notice 10 February 2026
summary: The Bank of England issued a US$2.5 billion 3-year eurobond (RegS/144A) on 10 February 2026 to finance its foreign currency reserves. The bond carries a 3.5% annual coupon and matures on 18 February 2029. This was the first operation under the Bank's updated issuance programme, which transitioned to targeting two benchmark issuances annually with a regular timetable and high transparency. The joint lead managers were RBC Capital Markets, BMO Capital Markets, HSBC Bank plc, and Morgan Stanley.
key_points: Issued a US$2.5 billion 3-year eurobond (RegS/144A) on 10 February 2026
Joint Lead Managers: RBC Capital Markets, BMO Capital Markets, HSBC Bank plc, and Morgan Stanley
First 2026 operation under the updated issuance programme announced September 2025
Bank shifted to two benchmark issuances annually with regular timetable and high degree of transparency
why_it_matters: This notice documents the Bank of England's structured approach to foreign currency reserve financing, providing transparency on issuance timing, size, and syndicate composition. The shift to a predictable two-benchmark annual issuance framework represents a change in market structure for how the Bank accesses wholesale funding markets for reserve management purposes.
market_structure_context: This notice documents a shift in the Bank of England's reserve financing strategy toward a more structured, predictable issuance timetable with designated lead managers. The Bank manages foreign exchange reserves separately from the Government's own reserves, with this financing mechanism supporting policy objectives.
notes: Source contains legal disclaimers and distribution restrictions typical of sovereign debt documentation. The page includes navigation links to other Bank of England content, but the main body contains substantive market notice details about the bond issuance. The 3.75% Bank Rate mentioned is from a related MPC announcement and not directly part of this notice.
